message:
fix(security): case-insensitive hostname matching in network_guard and ssrf_policy

DNS hostnames are case-insensitive per RFC 4343. network_guard's host_matches()
used exact string comparison, allowing deny-rule bypass via uppercase hostnames
(e.g. "EVIL.COM" would not match deny rule for "evil.com"). ssrf_policy's
find_allowlist() had the inverse issue — uppercase hosts would fail to match
allowlist entries, causing over-blocking.

- network_guard: normalize both pattern and host to ASCII lowercase
- ssrf_policy: use eq_ignore_ascii_case for allowlist host comparison
- Added regression tests for both modules

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
